name: Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark (MCSB) Ontology
description: Comprehensive ontology representing the Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark v1 framework, enabling AI agents to reason about cloud security controls, compliance mappings, implementation guidance, and responsibility assignments across Azure and multi-cloud environments. Acts as a governance overlay bridging RCSG security controls to EA architecture domains and Microsoft Cloud platform resources.

oaa:imports.name: EA-CORE-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Platform-agnostic EA core providing SecurityArchitecture for control-to-architecture domain mapping

oaa:imports.name: EA-MSFT-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Microsoft Cloud platform providing AzureResource for assessment targeting and CloudPlatform for platform-specific guidance filtering

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Security Control Architecture Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: MCSB security controls map to EA-CORE security architecture domains for enterprise security coverage analysis
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Map security controls to enterprise architecture security layers

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Control Assessment Resource Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: MCSB control assessments target specific Azure resources for resource-level security posture evaluation

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Implementation Guidance Platform Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: MCSB implementation guidance is tailored to specific cloud platforms for platform-filtered guidance retrieval

entities.name: ControlDomain
entities.description: A high-level security control category within the Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark that groups related security controls addressing a specific area of cloud security governance. MCSB v1 defines 12 control domains: Network Security (NS), Identity Management (IM), Privileged Access (PA), Data Protection (DP), Logging & Threat Detection (LT), Asset Management (AM), Governance & Strategy (GS), Incident Response (IR), Posture & Vulnerability Management (PV), Endpoint Security (ES), DevOps Security (DS), and Backup & Recovery (BR).

oaa:properties.name: domainCode
oaa:properties.description: Two-letter identifier for the control domain

oaa:properties.name: domainName
oaa:properties.description: Full name of the control domain

oaa:properties.name: controlCount
oaa:properties.description: Number of security controls within this domain

oaa:properties.name: defaultResponsibility
oaa:properties.description: Default shared responsibility model for the domain

entities.name: SecurityControl
entities.description: A specific security control within the MCSB framework identified by a unique control ID (e.g., NS-1, IM-3). Each control defines a security principle, implementation guidance, and compliance mappings to external frameworks. Controls are the atomic units of security governance.

oaa:properties.name: controlId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier in format DOMAIN-NUMBER (e.g., NS-1, IM-3, PA-7)

oaa:properties.name: securityPrinciple
oaa:properties.description: Core security principle or objective this control addresses

oaa:properties.name: severity
oaa:properties.description: Implementation priority severity level

oaa:properties.name: featureNotes
oaa:properties.description: Additional implementation notes and feature-specific guidance

entities.name: ImplementationGuidance
entities.description: Platform-specific implementation instructions for a security control, providing concrete steps, configuration guidance, and best practices for deploying the control on a specific cloud platform (Azure, AWS, or multi-cloud).

oaa:properties.name: guidanceText
oaa:properties.description: Detailed implementation guidance text with step-by-step instructions

oaa:properties.name: platformCode
oaa:properties.description: Target cloud platform identifier (e.g., Azure, AWS)

oaa:properties.name: azureServices
oaa:properties.description: Comma-separated list of relevant Azure services for this guidance

oaa:properties.name: awsServices
oaa:properties.description: Comma-separated list of relevant AWS services for this guidance

entities.name: ComplianceMapping
entities.description: A mapping between an MCSB security control and an external compliance framework control, establishing traceability for cross-framework compliance analysis. Supports NIST SP800-53, CIS Controls v7.1, CIS Controls v8, and PCI-DSS v3.2.1.

oaa:properties.name: mappedControlIds
oaa:properties.description: Comma-separated external framework control identifiers that map to this MCSB control

oaa:properties.name: mappingType
oaa:properties.description: Degree of mapping alignment (full, partial, related)

oaa:properties.name: mappingNotes
oaa:properties.description: Clarification of mapping scope or differences

entities.name: ComplianceFramework
entities.description: An external regulatory or industry compliance framework to which MCSB controls are mapped. Provides cross-framework traceability for multi-standard compliance programmes.

oaa:properties.name: frameworkCode
oaa:properties.description: Short code for the framework (e.g., NIST, CIS-v7, CIS-v8, PCI-DSS)

oaa:properties.name: frameworkName
oaa:properties.description: Full name of the compliance framework

oaa:properties.name: currentVersion
oaa:properties.description: Version of the framework mapped to

oaa:properties.name: governingBody
oaa:properties.description: Organisation responsible for maintaining the framework

entities.name: ResponsibilityAssignment
entities.description: A shared responsibility model assignment for a security control, defining whether the cloud provider, customer, or both are responsible for implementing and maintaining the control in a cloud deployment.

oaa:properties.name: responsibilityType
oaa:properties.description: Type of shared responsibility (Client, Provider, Joint, SolutionProvider)

oaa:properties.name: clientActions
oaa:properties.description: Actions the client/customer must take for this control

oaa:properties.name: providerActions
oaa:properties.description: Actions the cloud provider performs for this control

entities.name: SecurityStakeholder
entities.description: A security role or stakeholder function involved in the implementation, oversight, or governance of MCSB security controls. Used for RACI matrix generation and accountability mapping.

oaa:properties.name: stakeholderFunction
oaa:properties.description: Primary function or role title (e.g., CISO, Security Operations, Cloud Architect)

oaa:properties.name: raciRole
oaa:properties.description: RACI role for this stakeholder (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed)

entities.name: ControlAssessment
entities.description: An assessment of a security control's implementation status against MCSB requirements, tracking compliance posture, gaps, and remediation priorities for security posture management.

oaa:properties.name: assessmentStatus
oaa:properties.description: Current compliance status of the control

oaa:properties.name: assessmentDate
oaa:properties.description: Date the assessment was performed

oaa:properties.name: implementationGaps
oaa:properties.description: Description of gaps between current state and MCSB requirements

oaa:properties.name: remediationPriority
oaa:properties.description: Priority level for remediation of identified gaps

oaa:properties.name: assessor
oaa:properties.description: Person or team that performed the assessment

entities.name: SecurityArchitecture
entities.description: External EA-CORE-ONT entity representing cross-cutting security architecture domains that MCSB controls implement and govern.

entities.name: AzureResource
entities.description: External EA-MSFT-ONT entity representing Azure cloud resources targeted by MCSB control assessments.

entities.name: CloudPlatform
entities.description: External EA-MSFT-ONT entity representing cloud provider platforms for which MCSB provides implementation guidance.

relationships.name: belongsToDomain
relationships.description: A security control belongs to a control domain, establishing the primary categorisation of controls within the MCSB framework

relationships.name: hasControl
relationships.description: A control domain contains security controls that address its area of security governance

relationships.name: hasGuidance
relationships.description: A security control has platform-specific implementation guidance providing concrete deployment instructions

relationships.name: forControl
relationships.description: Implementation guidance is written for a specific security control

relationships.name: hasComplianceMapping
relationships.description: A security control has mappings to external compliance framework controls for cross-standard traceability

relationships.name: mapsToFramework
relationships.description: A compliance mapping references a specific external compliance framework

relationships.name: hasResponsibility
relationships.description: A security control has a shared responsibility assignment defining client and provider obligations

relationships.name: hasStakeholders
relationships.description: A security control has associated security stakeholders who participate in its governance via RACI roles

relationships.name: assessesControl
relationships.description: A control assessment evaluates a specific security control's implementation status

relationships.name: appliesTo
relationships.description: A security control applies to an EA-CORE security architecture domain, establishing which enterprise security layer the control implements

relationships.name: assessesResource
relationships.description: A control assessment targets a specific Azure resource for resource-level security posture evaluation

relationships.name: guidanceForPlatform
relationships.description: Implementation guidance is specific to a cloud platform, enabling platform-filtered guidance retrieval

enumerations.name: ResponsibilityType
enumerations.description: Shared responsibility model types for cloud security controls

hasDefinedTerm.name: Client
hasDefinedTerm.description: Customer is solely responsible for implementing and maintaining the control

hasDefinedTerm.name: Provider
hasDefinedTerm.description: Cloud provider is solely responsible for the control

hasDefinedTerm.name: Joint
hasDefinedTerm.description: Both customer and cloud provider share responsibility for the control

hasDefinedTerm.name: SolutionProvider
hasDefinedTerm.description: Third-party solution provider is responsible for the control

enumerations.name: MappingType
enumerations.description: Degree of alignment between MCSB controls and external framework controls

hasDefinedTerm.name: Full
hasDefinedTerm.description: Complete alignment — MCSB control fully maps to external framework control

hasDefinedTerm.name: Partial
hasDefinedTerm.description: Partial alignment — MCSB control partially covers external framework control

hasDefinedTerm.name: Related
hasDefinedTerm.description: Thematically related but not directly aligned

enumerations.name: AssessmentStatus
enumerations.description: Implementation compliance status for a security control assessment

hasDefinedTerm.name: Compliant
hasDefinedTerm.description: Control is fully implemented and meets MCSB requirements

hasDefinedTerm.name: PartiallyCompliant
hasDefinedTerm.description: Control is partially implemented with identified gaps

hasDefinedTerm.name: NonCompliant
hasDefinedTerm.description: Control is not implemented or does not meet MCSB requirements

hasDefinedTerm.name: NotApplicable
hasDefinedTerm.description: Control is not applicable to the assessed environment

hasDefinedTerm.name: NotAssessed
hasDefinedTerm.description: Control has not yet been assessed

enumerations.name: PriorityLevel
enumerations.description: Priority levels for security control remediation and implementation

hasDefinedTerm.name: Critical
hasDefinedTerm.description: Immediate remediation required — active security risk

hasDefinedTerm.name: High
hasDefinedTerm.description: Remediation within 30 days — significant security gap

hasDefinedTerm.name: Medium
hasDefinedTerm.description: Remediation within 90 days — moderate security improvement

hasDefinedTerm.name: Low
hasDefinedTerm.description: Remediation within next planning cycle — defence in depth enhancement

enumerations.name: ControlSeverity
enumerations.description: Implementation priority severity classification for security controls

hasDefinedTerm.name: Must_Have
hasDefinedTerm.description: Mandatory control — required for baseline security compliance

hasDefinedTerm.name: Nice_to_Have
hasDefinedTerm.description: Optional control — additional defence in depth

businessRules.name: Domain Code Uniqueness
businessRules.condition: IF ControlDomain exists THEN domainCode MUST be unique across all ControlDomain instances

businessRules.name: Control Domain Assignment
businessRules.condition: IF SecurityControl exists THEN it MUST have exactly one belongsToDomain relationship to a ControlDomain

businessRules.name: Compliance Mapping Completeness
businessRules.condition: IF SecurityControl exists THEN it MUST have at least one hasComplianceMapping relationship

businessRules.name: Responsibility Assignment Required
businessRules.condition: IF SecurityControl exists THEN it MUST have exactly one hasResponsibility relationship

businessRules.name: Joint Responsibility Actions
businessRules.condition: IF ResponsibilityAssignment has responsibilityType 'Joint' THEN both clientActions AND providerActions MUST be defined

businessRules.name: NonCompliant Gap Documentation
businessRules.condition: IF ControlAssessment has assessmentStatus 'NonCompliant' THEN implementationGaps MUST contain at least one gap description

businessRules.name: Gap Remediation Priority
businessRules.condition: IF ControlAssessment has non-empty implementationGaps THEN remediationPriority MUST be defined

businessRules.name: Guidance Platform Assignment
businessRules.condition: IF ImplementationGuidance exists THEN it SHOULD have a guidanceForPlatform relationship to a CloudPlatform

businessRules.name: Control ID Format Validation
businessRules.condition: IF SecurityControl exists THEN controlId MUST match pattern ^[A-Z]{2}-\d{1,2}$

businessRules.name: Security Architecture Coverage
businessRules.condition: IF SecurityControl exists THEN it SHOULD have at least one appliesTo relationship to SecurityArchitecture

crossOntologyReferences.description: MCSB-ONT is a governance overlay in RCSG-Series. Security controls map to EA-CORE SecurityArchitecture domains for enterprise coverage analysis. Control assessments target EA-MSFT AzureResources for resource-level security tracking. Implementation guidance is filtered by EA-MSFT CloudPlatform. EA-ONT (PPM) initiatives reference MCSB controls via the existing mitigatedByControl relationship.

keyBridges.name: Security Control to Architecture Bridge
keyBridges.purpose: Maps MCSB controls to enterprise security architecture domains for coverage analysis

keyBridges.name: Control Assessment to Azure Resource Bridge
keyBridges.purpose: Enables resource-level security assessment tracking against MCSB controls

keyBridges.name: Implementation Guidance Platform Bridge
keyBridges.purpose: Filters implementation guidance by target cloud platform

changeControl.changeNotes: Complete rebuild from OAA v5.0.0 to v6.1.0. Removed CloudPlatform and CloudService entities (now in EA-MSFT-ONT). Added 3 cross-ontology bridges to EA-CORE (SecurityArchitecture) and EA-MSFT (AzureResource, CloudPlatform). 8 owned entities, 12 relationships (9 internal + 3 cross-ontology), 10 business rules, 5 enumerations.

versionHistory.notes: Initial creation from MCSB v1 Excel source with 12 control domains, 76 controls, 4 compliance framework mappings. OAA v5.0.0.

OAA v6.1.0 rebuild: CloudPlatform/CloudService removed to EA-MSFT. Added 3 cross-ontology bridges (EA-CORE SecurityArchitecture, EA-MSFT AzureResource/CloudPlatform). Migrated to owl:ObjectProperty relationships with oaa:cardinality. 8 owned entities + 3 external stubs, 12 relationships, 10 business rules, 5 enumerations.
